title: LLM decoder PWL frontier detail
hypothesis: The two exact-safe PWL decoder survivors should be broken down by table footprint, interpolation width, normalization path, and integration risk before choosing an RTL/OpenROAD implementation anchor.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Between grid_approx_pwl_bf16_path and grid_approx_pwl_in_q8_w_q8_norm_exact, which path should be the immediate implementation anchor and what remains the open risk?
include: exact next-token and top-k quality gate
separate table, interpolation datapath, normalization, format-bridge, and integration-risk terms
follow_on_broad_sweep: prototype a bf16 reciprocal PWL softmax block estimate
prototype a q8 PWL exact-normalization block estimate
only add q8 reciprocal or bounded normalization if quality remains exact-safe

expected_benefit: turns the close PWL frontier into reviewable implementation-risk terms
prevents the q8 datapath advantage from hiding exact-normalization cost
keeps the bf16 primary-anchor decision provisional and tied to explicit evidence
risks: the detail model is still heuristic and not a replacement for RTL/OpenROAD evidence
normalization cost may move after a concrete block is implemented
quality conclusions still depend on the pinned tiny decoder prompt-stress setup
